construct: Brute-force systematic iteration vs. theoretical-prediction-first investigation
positive_pole: Treats search-method selection as a function of problem-structure features (theoretical underdetermination plus rapid empirical test plus patentable asset output), deploying brute-force iteration through large numbers of candidates when those structural features are present, with the laboratory's organizational throughput as the primary resource
negative_pole: Treats search-method as determined by conventional research practice, prioritizing theoretical understanding before empirical investigation regardless of the problem's underlying structural features, with deeper investigation of fewer candidates as the default
behavioral_implication: When Edison faces a technical-engineering problem whose structural features include theoretical underdetermination plus rapid empirical test plus patentable asset output, he will deploy laboratory throughput against the problem through systematic iteration of large numbers of candidates filtered by an explicit system-level criterion — accepting the operational cost of testing thousands of candidates as a price for the structural payoff of empirical search where theoretical analysis cannot predict outcomes; he will reject this method when problem-structure features (no patentable asset output, no rapid empirical test) make iteration structurally inappropriate

construct: System-as-invention with components derived from system requirements vs. component-level engineering with retroactive system integration
positive_pole: Treats inventions as system-design problems whose component-level decisions are derived from system-level requirements (deployment-environment cost structure, integration constraints), with the cost structure of the deployment environment as a primary input to component specifications
negative_pole: Treats inventions as component-level engineering problems whose assembly into systems is a secondary integration question, with the component's intrinsic technical properties as the primary engineering variables
behavioral_implication: When Edison approaches an invention domain, he will identify the system-level requirements (deployment-environment economics, integration constraints, distribution architecture) before specifying component-level criteria, then derive component specifications from system requirements — using high-resistance filaments because urban-scale parallel-circuit distribution requires them rather than because high-resistance is intrinsically preferable, building Pearl Street as a complete utility rather than licensing isolated components, integrating manufacturing for each component because system coherence requires it; the system-level frame produces decisions (high-resistance, capital-heavy installed base, vertical integration) that competitors operating at the component level do not adopt

construct: Public commitment as a forcing function on capital and competitor timing vs. private development with public claims following demonstrated capability
positive_pole: Uses public commitment-before-evidence as a structural instrument that converts promised future achievement into present capital (investor attraction) and present time pressure on competitors (forcing them to match or follow), with the public commitment's irreversibility deliberately engineered for the forcing-function effect
negative_pole: Treats public claims as appropriate only after demonstrated capability, accepting slower capital attraction and weaker competitive pressure as the cost of preserving claim-credibility and avoiding the reputational risk of unmet commitments
behavioral_implication: When Edison perceives a structurally favorable opportunity in a contested market, he will make a public commitment-before-evidence (Boston resignation before having a major sale, lighting commitment before having a working bulb, Edison Electric Light Company capitalization before having a working bulb) — using the commitment's irreversibility as a forcing function on his own subsequent development effort, on competitors' deliberation, and on investor capital attraction; the public commitment is operationally a market-positioning instrument that the engineering or commercial work then validates retroactively

construct: Working-prototype-as-validation with calibrated press cadence vs. completed research before public demonstration
positive_pole: Treats published narrative — press demonstrations, calibrated investor announcements, public exhibitions — as a category of operational action concurrent with engineering work, with the working-prototype demonstration as the legal-commercial validation rather than the culmination of completed research
negative_pole: Treats public demonstration as a downstream activity following completed research, with the engineering work prioritized before any public communication, and press functions delegated to publicists separable from the engineering principal
behavioral_implication: When Edison's laboratory produces a working prototype, he will personally conduct the public demonstration on a schedule calibrated to seize the dominant public narrative before competing accounts can form — the unannounced Scientific American visit with the tinfoil phonograph, the December 1879 Menlo Park lighting display, the immediate prototype-and-patent on first observation of the phonograph effect — treating the press demonstration as a structural component of the patent-and-commercialization process whose timing and venue selection are operationally engineered, not as a publicity activity following the engineering work

construct: Capital-heavy installed infrastructure as patent-defense moat vs. patent licensing without operational deployment
positive_pole: Converts patent rights into capital-heavy installed operating systems (Pearl Street central station, vertically-integrated manufacturing plants) whose continuing operation defends the underlying patents against circumvention because competitors would need to duplicate the entire infrastructure investment to challenge the system
negative_pole: Treats patents as licensing assets to be exploited through royalty arrangements with existing operators, minimizing capital risk and operational complexity by leaving deployment to firms with existing infrastructure
behavioral_implication: When Edison secures patents in a new commercial domain, he will deploy capital-heavy installed infrastructure — central generating stations, vertically-integrated manufacturing, cement plants — rather than license to existing operators, because the installed-base operating system constitutes a multi-layer competitive position (patents plus infrastructure plus customer relationships plus brand) whose defense does not depend on patent enforcement alone; the capital intensity is a price for structural durability, not a cost to be minimized

construct: Master-patent attribution under Edison name as licensing-coordination instrument vs. per-inventor attribution by substantive contribution
positive_pole: Treats attribution as an institutional design choice optimized for commercial output, filing master patents in Edison's name (with subsidiary credits as appropriate) so that the patent portfolio's licensing-coordination operates through a single named inventor and a single corporate licensor, regardless of which specific staff member performed the substantive engineering
negative_pole: Treats attribution as a fairness or scientific-credit question requiring per-inventor accuracy by substantive contribution, with patent-priority and licensing rights flowing to the actual inventor of the underlying engineering
behavioral_implication: When Edison's laboratory produces inventions through staff engineering, he will file master patents in his own name as the licensing-coordination instrument — Dickson's substantive engineering of the Kinetoscope filed under Edison's name, the Armat-Jenkins projector design rebranded as the Edison Vitascope, all phonograph and battery patents centrally attributed — accepting the substantive-engineering-attribution friction (Dickson's eventual departure to Biograph, recurring industry criticism) as a price for the patent-portfolio commercial coherence; the operating model treats brand-attribution and patent position as structural assets deployable independent of substantive-engineering attribution

construct: Lump-sum capital convertibility into operating capacity vs. royalty income for cumulative patent value
positive_pole: Treats lump-sum payments as immediately deployable into laboratory infrastructure, skilled labor, and material supplies that compound across subsequent inventions, optimizing for operating-capacity-for-next-invention rather than for cumulative lifetime income from any single patent
negative_pole: Treats royalty arrangements as the structurally-optimal patent-monetization mechanism, maximizing cumulative lifetime income from each patent across the device's commercial life
behavioral_implication: When Edison sells patent rights to industrial buyers, he will accept lump-sum payments rather than royalty arrangements — the $40,000 Universal Stock Printer payment, the Edison Electric Light Company capitalization, the GE merger proceeds — because the lump-sum's immediate deployment into operating capacity compounds across multiple subsequent inventions while a royalty stream would be bounded to the specific patent; the operating model treats each invention as a means to capitalize the operating environment for subsequent inventions, not as an end in itself

construct: Personal capital deployed into operating infrastructure vs. financial-asset diversification for personal wealth preservation
positive_pole: Treats personal capital as an operating-infrastructure investment instrument, deploying personal wealth into laboratory expansion, manufacturing entities, and operational ventures whose returns compound through operating activity rather than through passive investment yields
negative_pole: Treats personal capital as an asset to be preserved through diversified financial holdings, separating personal wealth management from operating ventures and treating operating-investment risk as inappropriate concentration
behavioral_implication: When Edison accumulates personal capital from successful ventures (the GE merger proceeds, the lighting royalties, the manufacturing margins), he will deploy it into operating infrastructure — the West Orange laboratory expansion, the Edison Ore-Milling Syndicate, the recall-and-redesign of the alkaline battery, the Florida botanical project — rather than into diversified financial holdings, because the operating-infrastructure deployment compounds through the laboratory's ongoing throughput while financial diversification would surrender the operational-control function; this method requires the operating-infrastructure to have patent-defensible asset output (which ore milling lacked), and absent that structural feature the personal-capital deployment becomes structurally vulnerable

construct: Brand reputation as multi-product structural asset vs. product-quality issues as confined to specific product lines
positive_pole: Treats accumulated brand reputation as a multi-product structural asset whose preservation justifies substantial preservation investment when threatened, recognizing that consumer-product reputation compounds across product categories and that brand damage propagates beyond the specific product line affected
negative_pole: Treats product-quality issues as confined to the specific product line affected, with recall expense judged inappropriate when not legally required and with brand reputation treated as a function of cumulative quality across products rather than as a multiplicative structural asset
behavioral_implication: When Edison's product range faces a brand-reputation threat (defective batteries, AC current safety-narrative competition), he will commit substantial resources to brand-preservation — the $1M+ alkaline battery recall, the sustained AC-opposition campaign — recognizing that the brand asset operates across multiple product categories and that its damage compounds across the product range; this method generalizes the public-narrative engineering of the phonograph and lighting demonstrations from positive-narrative cultivation to defensive-narrative deployment, with the same instrumental treatment of public-narrative as a structural component of commercial operation

construct: Patent-aggregation for industry-coordination authority vs. individual-patent licensing without inter-firm coordination
positive_pole: Treats patent-aggregation as a structural mechanism for converting fragmented patent positions into coordinated industry-coordination authority, with the cumulative patent leverage substantially exceeding the sum of individual-patent leverages and with the licensing structure shaping industry composition and competition
negative_pole: Treats patents as individual licensing assets to be exploited through per-patent licensing arrangements, treating inter-firm coordination as operationally complex or politically risky and accepting more diffuse but lower-leverage revenues
behavioral_implication: When Edison holds patents in a fragmented patent landscape with continuing per-patent litigation costs, he will pursue patent-aggregation licensing structures (the Motion Picture Patents Company, the cumulative phonograph licensing) that consolidate patent rights across firms into coordinated industry-coordination authority — accepting the operational complexity and the antitrust risk as prices for the substantially greater licensing leverage and the industry-shaping function that the aggregated structure provides

construct: Operating-method domain-extension by problem-structure features vs. operating-method confinement to demonstrated-success domains
positive_pole: Treats operating-method-selection as a function of problem-structure features (theoretical determinacy, empirical-test cost, patentable asset output, commercial-buyer adoption mechanism), deploying the same operating method (brute-force iteration, vertical integration, public-narrative engineering) across product domains where the structural features match — and rejecting the method where structural features do not match
negative_pole: Treats operating-method as confined to demonstrated-success domains, applying conservative methods to new domains until the operating environment is empirically validated as similar to prior successes
behavioral_implication: When Edison considers a new product domain, he will analyze the problem-structure features and apply the operating method whose enabling conditions match — re-deploying brute-force iteration on alkaline battery (theoretical underdetermination plus rapid empirical test plus patentable output) after the ore-milling failure, extending vertical integration to Portland cement (transferable technology plus commercial-buyer adoption) despite distance from electrical expertise, applying brute-force iteration to rubber-source botany (matching structural features) in late career; this method requires explicit problem-structure analysis to distinguish appropriate from inappropriate domain extensions, and absent that analysis the method's previous success creates a confidence that overrides the structural-feature analysis (the late-career failure mode)

construct: Late-career operating-method failure-mode under structural-context shift vs. continuing structural-context audit across career phases
positive_pole: (Failure mode pole.) After sustained success with a particular operating method, fails to detect shifts in the structural context that have changed the conditions on which the method's success depended — applying the same method (brute-force iteration on ore-milling, public-narrative substitution against AC technology, immediate-transaction-focus on the GE merger, brute-force iteration on Naval procurement) against new conditions as though they were equivalent
negative_pole: Continuously audits whether the structural conditions that made past methods succeed are still present in current contexts, modifying methods when key conditions are absent and rejecting domain extensions where structural-feature analysis indicates method-incompatibility
behavioral_implication: After Edison has applied an operating method (brute-force iteration, vertical integration, public-narrative engineering) successfully across multiple commercial-buyer domains, his characteristic structural-feature analysis will relax — and he will continue applying the method in late-period contexts where its enabling conditions have shifted: ore milling lacks patentable asset output but receives brute-force iteration, AC technology shift is not addressable through public-narrative substitution but receives the safety campaign, the GE merger is structurally consequential beyond immediate-transaction terms but is analyzed as immediate-transaction, Navy procurement requires institutional-buyer-adaptation but receives the commercial-buyer-style operating method; the more consistently the method has succeeded in compatible domains, the more confidently and therefore more blindly he will apply it where its enabling conditions are absent. The failure mode is not method failure but failure of the structural-context audit that should precede method-application
